# B006. Bayesian State Space Price-Vol Model

## Positioning

This candidate models price and volatility as a state-space system so that risk sizing can react before the drawdown arrives. It is a better fit for overlay logic than for pure alpha generation.

## Reference Basis

- [The Heston stochastic volatility model with piecewise constant parameters - efficient calibration and pricing of window barrier options](../../references/papers/1805.04704_heston_piecewise_constant.pdf) - volatility-regime calibration reference
- [Parameter Estimation of the Heston Volatility Model with Jumps in the Asset Prices](../../references/papers/2211.14814_heston_jumps.pdf) - jump-aware volatility reference

## Repo Anchors

### Operational Guardrails

Shared safety and execution rails used by every candidate before a signal can touch capital.
- [src/risk/manager.py](../../../src/risk/manager.py) - pre-trade risk gate and position haircut
- [src/brokers/paper.py](../../../src/brokers/paper.py) - paper-safe broker adapter
- [src/monitoring/health.py](../../../src/monitoring/health.py) - freshness, heartbeat, and kill-switch checks
- [src/backtest/engine.py](../../../src/backtest/engine.py) - deterministic offline backtest runner
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - queue-aware synthetic fill model
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - runtime health loop and stale-data monitor

### Market Data and Features

Point-in-time data loading plus the shared feature builder used by most signal stacks.
- [src/ingestion/loader.py](../../../src/ingestion/loader.py) - point-in-time market data loading
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - technical, lagged, and derived feature construction

### Regime and State

Regime detection and state overlays used when the signal should shrink or route rather than trade blind.
- [src/models/hmm.py](../../../src/models/hmm.py) - latent regime detector

## Data / Feature Bundle

- Build the state from price returns, realized volatility, and the standard feature matrix in `src/features/builder.py`.
- Treat the regime detector in `src/models/hmm.py` as a coarse prior when the volatility state becomes unstable.
- Keep the loader on point-in-time inputs so the state does not silently learn from future bars.

## Execution Path

- Forecast volatility one or more steps ahead and scale exposure accordingly.
- Use the state posterior as a drawdown haircut rather than as a hard entry signal.
- Let the same backtest engine compare the overlay against a rolling-vol baseline.

## Risk Controls

- Do not trust the model during jump clusters or after data outages.
- Reject stale realized-vol inputs and any state estimate with unstable posterior mass.
- Keep the overlay conservative: this is a sizing model, not a reason to lever up.

## Validation Gates

- Compare volatility forecast error against a rolling-window baseline.
- Check whether the overlay reduces tail losses on the same replay fixtures used by the smoke tests.
- Measure whether the model improves risk-adjusted return on top of F001/F023.

## Archive Triggers

- Archive if the overlay does not reduce drawdown or improve realized-vol accuracy.
- Archive if the state estimate is more brittle than the existing HMM overlay.
- Archive if the volatility signal does not survive transaction costs.

## Next Step

Use this as the volatility overlay reference for S003 and F041.
